title: The Effects of Blood Pressure Reduction on Cerebral Perfusion and Cognition in the Elderly Population
text:
Summary: The purpose of this study is to determine if a reduction in systolic blood pressure induced by anti-hypertensive medication results in changes in cerebral perfusion and cognition in hypertensive elderly. Hypertensive elderly will be treated using open-label anti-hypertensive medication for 8-12 weeks. Changes in cerebral perfusion and cognition will be assessed before and after treatment.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Age >= 70 years 

 Systolic office blood pressure >= 160 mmHg 

 Systolic home blood pressure >= 155 mmHg 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Diabetes Mellitus 

 Atrial fibrillation 

 Dementia 

 Renal failure requiring dialysis 

 Life expectancy of less than 1 year 

 Disabling stroke 

 Contraindication for MRI or anti-hypertensive medication 

 Systolic blood pressure > 220 mmHg
